CREATE TABLE ExampleTable (ID INT PRIMARY KEY, Name VARCHAR(100), Value VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO ExampleTable (ID, Name, Value) VALUES (1, 'Attribute1', 'Value1');
INSERT INTO ExampleTable (ID, Name, Value) VALUES (2, 'Attribute2', 'Value2');
INSERT INTO ExampleTable (ID, Name, Value) VALUES (3, 'Attribute3', 'Value3');
UPDATE ExampleTable SET Value = 'UpdatedValue1' WHERE ID = 1;
SELECT * FROM ExampleTable WHERE ID IN (SELECT ID FROM ExampleTable WHERE Name LIKE 'Attribute%');
SELECT E1.Name, E2.Value FROM ExampleTable E1 JOIN ExampleTable E2 ON E1.ID = E2.ID WHERE E1.Value <> E2.Value;
CREATE TABLE AnotherTable (AttrID INT, AttrValue VARCHAR(100), PRIMARY KEY (AttrID));
INSERT INTO AnotherTable (AttrID, AttrValue) SELECT ID, Value FROM ExampleTable WHERE ID > 1;
SELECT A.AttrValue FROM AnotherTable A WHERE A.AttrID IN (SELECT ID FROM ExampleTable WHERE Name = 'Attribute2');
CREATE TABLE ProfileSettings (ProfileID INT, SettingName VARCHAR(100), SettingValue VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO ProfileSettings (ProfileID, SettingName, SettingValue) VALUES (1, 'System', 'Value1');
INSERT INTO ProfileSettings (ProfileID, SettingName, SettingValue) VALUES (2, 'User', 'Value2');
SELECT * FROM ProfileSettings WHERE ProfileID IN (SELECT ProfileID FROM ProfileSettings WHERE SettingName = 'User');
UPDATE ProfileSettings SET SettingValue = 'UpdatedUserValue' WHERE ProfileID = 2 AND SettingName = 'User';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ProfileSettings WHERE SettingValue LIKE '%Value%';
